Empires & Puzzles Luna Review: The Crescent Owl of Mana Mayhem

Luna brings a unique mix of passive healing and aggressive mana reduction. But with her stats lagging behind the 2026 meta, is she a wise old owl or just a bird brain choice? We break down her viability.
Luna Review: This Owl Doesn't Give a Hoot About Your Mana
Luna: The Owl Who Asks "Who Needs Mana?"
Luna is a Legendary Dark (Purple) hero from the
family, originally released during Challenge Festival II in late 2023. As a

, she specializes in disrupting enemy plans while keeping her team lightly topped up. She’s a "Fast" mana speed hero who doesn't just hit you once and leave; she lingers like a bad smell—or in this case, a persistent moonbeam—chipping away at your health and mana for multiple turns. While she was once a terror on defense, the sands of time (and power creep) have shifted the landscape significantly.

Luna is a visually striking "Crescent Owl" wrapped in a mystical purple cloak with glowing runes. She looks like she knows the secrets of the universe and is slightly judging you for your tile matching skills. The art perfectly captures the "mystical pet" vibe—cute but undeniably powerful and magical. It’s a great design that stands out even among the weird and wonderful menagerie of the Mighty Pets family.
Let's be brutally honest: Luna's base stats are showing their age. With a base Power of 938, she is sitting over
than the modern titans released in late 2025 (who are pushing ~1260 Power).
Significantly lower than modern attackers who boast 1300+.
Her bulk is her biggest liability. Modern 5-stars are walking around with 2200+ HP and 1300+ Defense.
She is incredibly fragile by today's standards. Without double limit breaking (and arguably even with it), she risks being one-shot by modern snipers before she can even hoot.

of the turn, often undoing the mana gained from slash attacks or tiles. Even spread out, it disrupts timing constantly.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Mighty Pets family adds significant value to Luna, especially in minion/fiend heavy metas.
The bonus provides extra stats to Minions (HP/Attack) and protection against Fiends (making them weaker). It's situational unless you pair her with summoners, which you should.

This is excellent. If you pair Luna with a fast minion summoner (like many Christmas heroes or other Mighty Pets), she gains free HP and Mana every turn. It effectively makes her speed "Very Fast" in the right team.
A nice counter-punch to Fiend summoners. Dealing 85% damage to all enemies just because an opponent summoned a Fiend is a great "passive-aggressive" punishment.

for the first 6 turns is top-tier, especially for a healer/supporter. It guarantees she won't be Silenced, Mana Blocked, or Defense Broken right out of the gate, ensuring she almost always gets her first cast off.
Upon release, Luna was a menace. Her constant mana cutting made her a staple in top-tier defenses, particularly in the tank or flank position. She was the "clock-blocker" of choice.

Her stats have fallen off a cliff. The damage from her Moon Beams (140%) is based on her Attack stat, which is now low, meaning the beams tickle modern heroes rather than sting. The 20% heal is negligible against modern damage dealers who hit for 600%+.
She has moved from a "Universal Defense Staple" to a
. She is too squishy for modern war defense but remains excellent in
wars/tournaments (where her mana control is oppressive) or
(where the damage/mana cut works even if healing is blocked).
Current status: Luna does not have a costume.
If she receives one in the future, it desperately needs to address her base stats to bring her survivability up to 2026 standards.
She needs every ounce of bulk to survive modern snipers. Her primary role is to stay alive to keep the Moon Beams active.
Her Moon Beam damage (scaled off Attack) will be slightly lower, but a dead owl deals zero damage.

Extremely low durability by modern standards.
You cannot control who the Moon Beams hit. They might hit a nearly dead enemy or a full mana enemy you wanted to cut—it's a gamble.
The "Moon Beam" buff on her allies can be dispelled. If the enemy has a dispeller, her utility vanishes.
She only buffs herself and neighbors (3 heroes max), limiting the total output compared to team-wide buffers.
Her mana control is devastating when she fires even faster or when preventing Very Fast enemies from nuking you.
The "damage + mana cut" mechanic is highly valuable here, even if the heal is wasted.
Great for auto-farming as the beams clean up weakened waves automatically.
(e.g., modern Christmas heroes or other Mighty Pets).
Her passive gives her 5% Mana every time a minion is summoned. If you have a summoner firing, Luna charges significantly faster, potentially firing in 6 tiles or less.
While enemies try to dispel Luna's buff, they often trigger counter-attacks. The mana cut from Luna prevents them from healing up the counter-attack damage.
Any hero who dispels (Sabina, Caedmon, or modern equivalents) instantly neutralizes her main threat.
Heroes who prevent buffs (e.g., Mist, Franz) stop her from applying the Moon Beam status.
While not a direct counter to her skill, they neutralize her passive mana gain if you rely on minions.

. Her fast speed and mana control will carry you through map stages and raids well into the mid-game. She teaches you the value of mana control over raw damage.
. Do not put her on defense in top 100 wars; she will be farmed. However, keep her for

or as a counter to slower, heavy-hitting teams where you can manually control the mana flow. She is likely not worth an Alpha Aether anymore unless you have a specific surplus or use her daily in a specialized team.
"I really hated facing her before, but it seems like she isn't as scary now, is she?"
- A sentiment shared by many, marking her transition from "Terrifying" to "Manageable".
"Just like that, you can make use of any enemy flaws... but Luna's random targeting can sometimes be a genius play or a total flop."
Many players initially found the "20% heal" laughable, until they realized they never got to cast their own specials because of the constant mana cuts.

Luna is the perfect example of a hero who aged gracefully in utility but poorly in physique. Her mechanics—automatic damage and mana cutting—are timelessly annoying and effective. However, her stats are from a bygone era. She's like a vintage sports car: beautiful, fun to drive on weekends, but you wouldn't want to take her into a demolition derby against modern monster trucks.
for roster depth and specific tournaments, but no longer an S-tier contender. Keep her, love her, but protect her.
Is Luna still worth ascending in 2026? We analyze her unique Moon Beam mechanics, fast mana speed, and Mighty Pet passives in this comprehensive review.
